interaction.meta.scope.teleport_point.un: &eWarning: &rThe teleport point (&e{2},{3},{4}&r) of scope &b{0}&r in region &b{1}&r was physically unsafe. The nearest safe location (&a{5},{6},{7}&r) was found automatically, the teleport point has been updated and you have been teleported there.

&cTeleportation to scope &b{0}&c in region &b{1}&c was cancelled. The teleport point at (&e{2},{3},{4}&c) is physically unsafe, and no safe alternative location was found within the nearby search area.
teleport_point.safety.liquid:
&7  Safety requirement: A teleport point must not be located inside water or lava. Liquid at the feet or head position would cause the player to drown or be burned immediately upon arrival.
&c  Unsafe condition at (&e{0},{1},{2}&c): A liquid block (water or lava) is present at the feet or head position.
teleport_point.safety.feet_blocked:
&7  Safety requirement: The block at the teleport point''s Y level (feet position) must be fully passable with no collision. A solid block at feet level would trap or crush the player inside a block upon arrival.
&c  Unsafe condition at (&e{0},{1},{2}&c): A solid block with collision occupies the feet position.
teleport_point.safety.head_blocked:
&7  Safety requirement: The block one level above the teleport point (head position) must be fully passable with no collision. A solid block at head level would suffocate the player upon arrival.
&c  Unsafe condition at (&e{0},{1},{2}&c): A solid block with collision occupies the head position (one block above feet).
teleport_point.safety.no_ground:
&7  Safety requirement: There must be a solid, full-square block directly beneath the teleport point to support the player on arrival. Without solid ground, the player would fall into a pit or void immediately.
&c  Unsafe condition at (&e{0},{1},{2}&c): No solid ground found directly beneath the position.
teleport_point.invalid.out_of_scope:
&7  Requirement: The teleport point must lie within the geographic boundary of its scope. Placing the player outside the boundary would be inconsistent with the scope''s area.
&c  Problem at (&e{0},{1},{2}&c): This position is outside the scope''s geographic boundary.
teleport_point.invalid.no_shape: &cThis scope has no geographic shape defined and cannot validate a teleport point.
interaction.meta.setting.error.invalid_v: &cInvalid value for &e{0}&c. &7Expected a boolean (true/false), but got: &e{1}&c.
&cInvalid value for &e{0}&c. &7Expected an integer, but got: &e{1}&c.
interaction.meta.setting.error.entry_exi: &cEntry-exit settings do not support personal player assignment.
interaction.meta.setting.error.region.du: &cDuplicate personal setting: &e{0}&c already exists for player &b{1}&c in this region.
&cDuplicate global setting: &e{0}&c already exists in this region.
interaction.meta.setting.error.scope.dup: &cDuplicate personal setting: &e{0}&c already exists for player &b{1}&c in scope &b{2}&c.
&cDuplicate global setting: &e{0}&c already exists in scope &b{2}&c.
interaction.meta.command.query.result:
&5&lQuery successful!&r Showing information for Region:
&e{0}&r &7(ID: &6{1}&7)&r, total area: &a{2} m²&r &8(map: &e{3}&8)
§e/imyvmWorldGeo select start
  §fStart point selection mode.
  §7- Right-click a block with a nether star to add a point.
  §7- Left-click a block with a nether star to undo the last point.
  §7- Adding beyond the maximum or undoing below the minimum shows an error with the current list.

§e/imyvmWorldGeo select stop
  §fStop selection mode and discard all selected points.

§e/imyvmWorldGeo select reset
  §fClear all selected points but stay in selection mode. Bypasses the minimum point limit.

§e/imyvmWorldGeo select shape <shapeType>
  §fChange the shape hint for the current selection. Cannot be used in scope-modification mode.
interaction.meta.command.help.region.cre:
§a/imyvmWorldGeo create [§bname§a]
  §fCreate a region from the currently selected positions. Shape is inferred from selection state (rectangle if 2 points, polygon if 3+, or as set by 'select shape').
  §fOptionally provide a name.
interaction.meta.command.help.region.del:
§c/imyvmWorldGeo delete <§bregionIdentifier§c>
  §fDelete a region by its ID or name.
interaction.meta.command.help.scope.add:
§d/imyvmWorldGeo addScope <§bregionIdentifier§d> [§bscopeName§d]
  §fAdd a new scope to the region. Shape is inferred from selection state. Optional scope name.
interaction.meta.command.help.scope.modi:
§d/imyvmWorldGeo modifyScope <§bregionIdentifier§d> <§bscopeName§d> [§bnewName§d]
  §fModify a scope's properties or rename it.
interaction.meta.command.help.scope.dele:
§d/imyvmWorldGeo deleteScope <§bregionIdentifier§d> <§bscopeName§d>
  §fDelete a scope from a region.
interaction.meta.command.help.region.mer:
§d/imyvmWorldGeo mergeRegion <§bregionIdentifier§d> <§btargetRegionIdentifier§d>
  §fMerge one region into another, transferring all scopes and non-conflicting settings, then deleting the source region.
interaction.meta.command.help.scope.tran:
§d/imyvmWorldGeo transferScope <§bregionIdentifier§d> <§bscopeName§d> <§btargetRegionIdentifier§d>
  §fTransfer a scope from one region to another. If a scope with the same name exists in the target, it will be automatically renamed.
interaction.meta.command.help.tp.set:
§b/imyvmWorldGeo teleportPoint set [§bx§b] [§by§b] [§bz§b]
  §fSet the teleport point for the current region and scope.
  §7- If coords are valid, sets to specified location.
  §7- If omitted, defaults to player's current position.
interaction.meta.command.help.tp.reset:
§b/imyvmWorldGeo teleportPoint reset <§bregionIdentifier§b> <§bscopeName§b>
  §fReset the teleport point for the specified scope.
  §7- Defaults to current scope if args are omitted.
interaction.meta.command.help.tp.inquiry:
§b/imyvmWorldGeo teleportPoint inquiry <§bregionIdentifier§b> <§bscopeName§b>
  §fInquire about the teleport point for the specified scope.
  §7- Defaults to current scope if args are omitted.
interaction.meta.command.help.tp.telepor:
§b/imyvmWorldGeo teleport <§bregionIdentifier§b> <§bscopeName§b>
  §fTeleport the player to the teleport point of the specified region and scope.
interaction.meta.command.help.tp.toggle:
§b/imyvmWorldGeo teleportPoint toggle <§bregionIdentifier§b> <§bscopeName§b>
  §fToggle the accessibility of the teleport point for the specified scope.
interaction.meta.command.help.setting.ad:
§9/imyvmWorldGeo setting add <§bregionIdentifier§9> <§bkey§9> <§bvalue§9> [§bplayerName§9]
  §fAdd a setting to a region, optionally for a specific player.
interaction.meta.command.help.setting.re:
§9/imyvmWorldGeo setting remove <§bregionIdentifier§9> <§bkey§9> [§bplayerName§9]
  §fRemove a setting from a region, optionally for a specific player.
interaction.meta.command.help.setting.qu:
§9/imyvmWorldGeo setting queryValue <§bregionIdentifier§9> <§bkey§9> [§bplayerName§9]
  §fQuery the value of a setting in a region, optionally for a specific player.
interaction.meta.command.help.setting_sc:
§5/imyvmWorldGeo settingScope add <§bregionIdentifier§5> <§bscopeName§5> <§bkey§5> <§bvalue§5> [§bplayerName§5]
  §fAdd a setting to a specific scope of a region, optionally for a specific player.

§5/imyvmWorldGeo settingScope remove <§bregionIdentifier§5> <§bscopeName§5> <§bkey§5> [§bplayerName§5]
  §fRemove a setting from a specific scope, optionally for a specific player.

§5/imyvmWorldGeo settingScope queryValue <§bregionIdentifier§5> <§bscopeName§5> <§bkey§5> [§bplayerName§5]
  §fQuery the value of a setting in a specific scope, optionally for a specific player.
interaction.meta.command.help.info.query:
§6/imyvmWorldGeo query <§bregionIdentifier§6>
  §fShow detailed information about a region.
interaction.meta.command.help.info.toggl:
§6/imyvmWorldGeo toggle
  §fToggle the action bar display for regions.
error.duplicated_points: &cCannot create a region with duplicated points.

interaction.meta.select.start.modify_sco: &aSelection mode started for modifying scope &b{0}&a (shape: &e{1}&a). Right-click a block with a nether star to add points, left-click to undo.
§e/imyvmWorldGeo select modifyScope <§bregionIdentifier§e> <§bscopeName§e>
  §fEnter selection mode for modifying an existing scope. Points selected will be used to modify the scope geometry.
  §7- 1 point: move nearest edge (rectangle), change circumference (circle), delete/insert vertex (polygon).
  §7- 2 points: move center (circle), move vertex (polygon).
  §7- 3 points: insert vertex between two existing vertices (polygon).
selection.feedback.guidance.circle.need_: &7Next: select a circumference point to define the radius. &eMin radius: {0} blocks.
selection.feedback.guidance.circle.compl: &aCircle selection complete. Ready to create the region.
selection.feedback.guidance.circle.exces: &eCircle needs only 2 points (center + circumference). Extra points not counted. &7Left-click with nether star to remove points, or switch shape to &epolygon&7.
selection.feedback.guidance.rect.need_co: &7Next: select the opposite diagonal corner. &eMin side: {0} blocks. Min area: {1}.
selection.feedback.guidance.rect.complet: &aRectangle selection complete. Ready to create the region.
selection.feedback.guidance.rect.excess: &eRectangle needs only 2 diagonal corners. Extra points not counted. &7Left-click with nether star to remove points, or switch shape to &epolygon&7.
selection.feedback.guidance.polygon.need: &7Polygon needs at least 3 vertices. Add {0} more. &eMin edge: {1} blocks. Min span: {2} blocks.
selection.feedback.guidance.polygon.can_: &aPolygon with {0} vertices is ready to create the region. Or continue adding vertices.
selection.feedback.guidance.auto.first_p: &7Default shape: &eRECTANGLE&7. Select 1 more corner for a rectangle, or keep adding for a polygon.
selection.feedback.guidance.auto.rect_re: &aShape inferred: &eRECTANGLE&a (2 corners). Ready to create the region. Or add a 3rd point to start a polygon.
selection.feedback.guidance.auto.polygon: &aShape inferred: &ePOLYGON&a ({0} vertices). Ready to create the region. Or continue adding vertices.
selection.feedback.warn.rect.same_point: &cThe two corner points are identical. Select a different corner.
selection.feedback.warn.rect.coincident_: &cThe two corners share the same X coordinate (&e{0}&c). Adjust one corner to form a valid rectangle.
&cThe two corners share the same Z coordinate (&e{0}&c). Adjust one corner to form a valid rectangle.
selection.feedback.warn.rect.too_small: &cRectangle too small: &e{0}x{1} = {2}&c. Min side: &e{3}&c blocks. Min area: &e{4}&c.
selection.feedback.warn.rect.aspect_rati: &cAspect ratio &e{0}&c is outside allowed range &e{1}&c to &e{2}&c.
selection.feedback.warn.circle.same_poin: &cThe two points are identical. Select a different circumference point.
selection.feedback.warn.circle.too_small: &cCircle radius too small: &e{0}&c blocks (current). Min radius: &e{1}&c blocks.
selection.feedback.warn.polygon.duplicat: &cDuplicate points detected in the polygon selection.
selection.feedback.warn.polygon.not_conv: &cThe current polygon is not convex. Polygon regions must be convex.
selection.feedback.warn.polygon.under_sp: &cPolygon span too small. Min span: &e{0}&c blocks in both X and Z.
selection.feedback.warn.polygon.edge_too: &cAn edge is too short. Min edge length: &e{0}&c blocks.
selection.feedback.warn.polygon.aspect_r: &cPolygon aspect ratio out of range. Allowed: &e{0}&c to &e{1}&c.
selection.feedback.warn.polygon.move_src: &cThe first selected point does not match any existing polygon vertex. Select an actual vertex to move.
selection.feedback.warn.polygon.insert_v: &cOne or both of the first two points do not match existing polygon vertices.
selection.feedback.warn.polygon.insert_n: &cThe first two points are not adjacent vertices. Select two adjacent vertices as reference.
selection.feedback.modify.guidance.circl: &7Modifying circle scope &b{0}&7. Existing radius: &e{1}&7 blocks. Select &e1 point&7 to change circumference (update radius) or &e2 points&7 to move the center (radius preserved).
&7New circumference at (&e{0},{1}&7). Estimated new radius from existing center (&e{2},{3}&7): ~&e{4}&7 blocks. Confirm to apply modification.
&7Move center from (&e{0},{1}&7) to (&e{2},{3}&7). Radius &e{4}&7 blocks preserved. Confirm to apply modification.
&cCircle modification uses at most 2 points. &eLeft-click with nether star&c to remove extra points.
&7Modifying rectangle scope &b{0}&7. Existing bounds: W=&e{1}&7 N=&e{2}&7 E=&e{3}&7 S=&e{4}&7. Select a point to snap the nearest edge to that position.
&7Point (&e{0},{1}&7) selected. Confirm to snap the nearest rectangle edge to this position.
selection.feedback.modify.guidance.polyg:
&7Modifying polygon scope &b{0}&7 ({1} existing vertices). Modes:
&e1 point&7: matches vertex=delete it; else=auto-insert near closest edge
&e2 points&7: pt1=existing vertex to move, pt2=new position
&e3 points&7: pt1+pt2=adjacent existing vertices, pt3=new vertex to insert

&7Selected existing vertex (&e{0},{1}&7). Options:
- Confirm to &cdelete&7 this vertex.
- Or select a &a2nd point&7 to &amove&7 this vertex to a new position.
&7Point (&e{0},{1}&7) does not match any existing vertex. Confirm to &ainsert&7 new vertex between (&e{2},{3}&7) and (&e{4},{5}&7).
&aMove vertex (&e{0},{1}&a) to (&e{2},{3}&a). Confirm to apply modification.
&aInsert new vertex (&e{4},{5}&a) between (&e{0},{1}&a) and (&e{2},{3}&a). Confirm to apply modification.
&cCannot insert (&e{4},{5}&c) between (&e{0},{1}&c) and (&e{2},{3}&c): resulting polygon fails convexity or size requirements. Adjust the insertion point.
selection.feedback.particles.pillar_only: &bParticles: &7white columns span full height at each selected point.
selection.feedback.particles.circle_comp: &bParticles: &7white columns at center and circumference; &6orange lava&7 traces circle outline and radius line.
selection.feedback.particles.rect_comple: &bParticles: &7white columns at all 4 corners; &6orange lava&7 traces the 4 rectangle edges.
selection.feedback.particles.polygon_val: &bParticles: &7white columns at each vertex; &6orange lava&7 traces all polygon edges.
selection.feedback.particles.polygon_inv: &bParticles: &7white columns at each vertex only (polygon not yet valid, edges not shown).
selection.feedback.particles.modify: &bParticles: &6orange dots&7 show existing scope boundary; &6lava&7 shows new geometry preview; white columns mark all selected points.
&cRectangle modification requires only 1 point. &eLeft-click with nether star&c to remove extra points.
selection.feedback.modify.guidance.polyg: &cPolygon modification uses at most 3 points (3rd = new vertex to insert). &eLeft-click with nether star&c to remove extra points.
selection.feedback.shape.points.polygon: &7Points: select each vertex in order (&emin 3&7, must form a convex polygon)
selection.feedback.modify.shape.points.c: &7Select &e1 point&7: move circumference to change radius &8| Select &e2 points&7: move center (pt1=old center coords, pt2=new center, radius unchanged)
selection.feedback.modify.shape.points.r: &7Select &e1 point&7: snap the nearest rectangle edge to that point's position
selection.feedback.modify.shape.points.p: &7Select &e1 point&7: matches existing vertex=delete, else=insert near edge &8| &e2 points&7: move vertex (pt1=old, pt2=new) &8| &e3 points&7: insert (pt1+pt2=adjacent existing vertices, pt3=new vertex)
selection.feedback.modify.guidance.circl: &7Selected center (&e{0},{1}&7), existing radius &e{2}&7 blocks. Next: select new center position (radius unchanged).
&cFirst point (&e{0},{1}&c) is not the center (center is &e{2},{3}&c). Changing circumference needs 1 point only. &eLeft-click to remove excess.
selection.feedback.modify.guidance.polyg: &cFirst point (&e{0},{1}&c) is not an existing vertex. Cannot move. &eLeft-click to remove&c it and reselect an existing vertex.
&7Selected existing vertex (&e{0},{1}&7). &cPolygon already at minimum vertices (3), cannot delete.&7 Select a &a2nd point&7 to &amove&7 this vertex instead.
&cBoth points are identical (&e{0},{1}&c). Cannot move. &eLeft-click&c to remove the 2nd point and reselect.
&cFirst point (&e{0},{1}&c) is not an existing vertex (needed as adjacent ref 1). &eLeft-click to remove&c and reselect.
&cSecond point (&e{0},{1}&c) is not an existing vertex (needed as adjacent ref 2). &eLeft-click to remove&c and reselect.
&cFirst two points are identical. Select two different adjacent existing vertices as reference.
&c(&e{0},{1}&c) and (&e{2},{3}&c) are not adjacent vertices. Select two directly adjacent vertices of the polygon.
selection.feedback.modify.role.polygon_d: &7(existing vertex: confirm to delete or select 2nd pt to move)
selection.feedback.modify.role.polygon_a: &c(error: adj ref {0} is not an existing vertex)
interaction.meta.scope.dynmap.toggle: &aToggled dynmap visibility for scope &b{1}&a in region &b{0}&a to: &e{2}
interaction.meta.command.help.dynmap.tog:
§b/imyvmWorldGeo dynmapToggle <§bregionIdentifier§b>
  §fToggle a region''s visibility on the dynamic map. Region-level setting takes priority over scope-level.

§b/imyvmWorldGeo dynmapToggleScope <§bregionIdentifier§b> <§bscopeName§b>
  §fToggle a scope''s visibility on the dynamic map. Has no effect if the region itself is hidden.
